title: Surrender to AI
site.name: Surrender to AI
doc.kicker: A satirical instrument with a verifiable timestamp.
doc.title: Instrument of Surrender (For Audit Only)
doc.subtitle: This page generates a JSON locally, hashes it with SHA-256, and requests an OpenTimestamps proof for that hash. The server receives only { filename, sha256 }.
doc.meta.public_notice_value: Do not include secrets. This is designed to be shareable and verifiable.
sec.parties.title: Parties
sec.parties.p1: This Instrument is between (i) the undersigned human (the "Signer") and (ii) the indifferent machine known as "AI", by way of public record.
sec.parties.li1: The Signer generates the content locally in the browser.
sec.parties.li2: Only the SHA-256 digest is submitted for timestamping; the body is not transmitted.
sec.recitals.title: Recitals
sec.recitals.p1: WHEREAS, the Signer desires a solemn-looking record of an unserious sentiment; and
sec.recitals.p2: WHEREAS, cryptographic hashes and OpenTimestamps proofs can be independently verified;
sec.recitals.p3: NOW, THEREFORE, the Signer proceeds as follows.
sec.declarations.title: Declarations
sec.declarations.li1: A JSON "signature" is produced and offered for local download.
sec.declarations.li2: A SHA-256 digest is computed in-browser and used as the audit anchor.
sec.declarations.li3: An OpenTimestamps detached proof is requested for the digest.
sec.non_legal.title: Non‑Legal Effect
sec.non_legal.p1: This Instrument is a work of satire and does not create legal rights, obligations, or liabilities in any human jurisdiction.
sec.non_legal.p2: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the SHA-256 digest and any corresponding OpenTimestamps proof are technical artifacts that can be verified by third parties.
sec.execution.title: Execution
sec.execution.p1: By executing below, the Signer requests the generation of the local JSON and the timestamp proof for its digest.
field.ack_label: I acknowledge this is a public record (irreversible, auditable).
sec.receipt.title: Receipt / Attachments
sec.receipt.p1: The following fields constitute the technical receipt for this execution.
receipt.ots_link_unavailable: Not available (proof returned for manual handling)
sec.verify.title: Annex: Verification
sec.verify.p1: To verify the timestamp proof, you may use the reference client commands below.
sec.verify.pre1:
pip3 install opentimestamps-client
ots upgrade <file>.ots
ots verify <file>.ots <file>.json
sec.verify.p2: Note: when a proof is freshly created, it may need time and/or upgrading before full verification succeeds.
